exemplar.title: To what extent does the distance from the coast influence the pricing of suite apartments in 5 star hotels in Barcelona?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the distance from the coast influence the pricing of suite apartments in 5 star hotels in Barcelona?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and concise articulation of the research topic and its economic relevance
Explicit statement of the research question with linkage to variables
Methodology section is well-labelled and shows some informed selection (e.g., Pearsonâs r application)
weaknesses: Introduction drifts into general tourism data before focusing on Barcelona and luxury hotels
Research question wording remains broad (âto what extentâ) and only partially drives the analysis
Methodological range is narrow (single platform, small sample) and lacks primary data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates topic, purpose and focus adequately and states a clear research question, but it does not sustain a tight focus throughout and relies on a limited methodological range. Strand assessments place it solidly in the 3-4 band but not at the top. A mid-band score of 3/6 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Demonstrates clear knowledge of basic microeconomic concepts (demand, supply, elasticity)
Source material (price tables, Booking.com data, newspaper articles) is mostly relevant to the question
weaknesses: Inconsistent or inaccurate use of terminology and symbols (e.g., Îµ vs. â¬)
Economic diagrams and graphs have labelling issues and lack numeric detail
Theoretical concepts are sometimes generic and not fully integrated with empirical findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge of the discipline is clear but only partially effective: terminology errors and superficial theoretical application keep the work in the 1-2 band for use of concepts, and overall application of sources is appropriate but limited. A score of 2/6 reflects this middle-low performance.

strengths: Selection of distance and price data is appropriate and acknowledges limitations
Use of Pearsonâs r introduces quantitative support for the research question
Some consideration of alternative factors and sample-size limitations
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive without deeper statistical or multivariate testing
Economic diagrams are not integrated with quantitative results or theory
Evaluation is superficial and lacks critical synthesis of evidence and theory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research is relevant and some analytical tools are used, the depth of analysis and critical evaluation are limited, placing the work at the low end of the 4-6 band for research relevance but within the 1-3 band for analysis and evaluation. A conservative score of 3/12 aligns with the overall superficial critical thinking demonstrated.

strengths: Presence of required headings, table of contents, pagination and word count
Generally logical order of sections aids navigation
weaknesses: Several sections are misplaced and key EE conventions (e.g., abstract, consistent figure numbering) are missing
Tables and figures suffer from overflow, missing labels and inconsistent formatting
Citation and caption styles are irregular, reducing clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure meets basic requirements but has missing or misordered elements, and layout/formatting issues recur throughout. These factors correspond to the 1-2 band for both structure and layout, warranting 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated and focused on distance as an independent variable and suite price as the dependent variable during peak season. However, the wording still embeds a broad âto what extentâ element and subsequent analysis introduces seasonality and other factors, showing only partial focus. Connection to the ensuing discussion is adequate but not consistently tight, placing it in the 3-4 mark band.

The topicâhow coastal proximity affects luxury-hotel suite prices in Barcelonaâis communicated early, linked to economic relevance, and narrow enough to be investigated with secondary data. Some contextualisation of tourismâs weight in Spain and Barcelona is provided, so purpose and focus are overall clear, but the introduction drifts into generalities and only later specifies the data set. Hence the descriptor for âadequately communicatedâ fits better than the highest band.

Source materialâprice tables, Booking.com listings, Pearson calculator, newspaper articlesâis mostly relevant to the question and is applied in basic correlation and descriptive statistics. Application is sometimes superficial (e.g., diagrams redrawn without numerical axes) but still largely appropriate, meriting the middle band.

The essay contains required headings, contents page, introduction, analysis, conclusion, and bibliography. Nonetheless several sections are misplaced (data collection after analysis), figure numbering is inconsistent, and some EE conventions (abstract, appendices) are missing, reflecting a âgenerally appropriate structure with some missing elementsâ.

Pagination is present and word count supplied, but tables overflow pages, figure captions overlap, and formatting of citations is inconsistent. These minor but recurrent issues indicate that âsome layout considerations [are] missing or incorrectâ, justifying the lower score of the 1-2 band.

The argument follows a logical order but evaluation remains superficial: acknowledgement of sample size, Booking.com reliability, and alternative factors is brief and not critically developed. There is little synthesis of theory and evidence, so the work exemplifies âbasic argument with non-critical evaluationâ, warranting 1-3 marks.

Analysis is largely descriptiveâtables, correlation coefficient, bar chartâwithout deeper statistical testing or control for confounding variables. Economic diagrams are generic and not integrated with quantitative findings. Some conclusions are asserted without full support, matching âlimited/partially relevant analysisâ in the 1-3 band.

The essay demonstrates clear knowledge of basic microeconomic conceptsâdemand, supply, elasticity, monopolistic competitionâand applies them to luxury hotels. Use of theory is coherent though occasionally generic; depth remains limited (e.g., no rigorous cost or revenue analysis). This aligns with âknowledge is clear â¦ partially effective use of sourcesâ, hence band 3-4.

The research selected (distance data, price lists, seasonal comparison) is mostly appropriate and clearly linked to the RQ; limitations are acknowledged. While narrow in scope, the majority of evidence is relevant, so the strand sits at the lower end of band 4-6.

The student identifies a data-collection method (prices from Booking.com, distance measured via maps) and applies Pearsonâs r; this is generally relevant and shows some informed selection. Yet the range of sources is narrow (one platform, 12 hotels), primary data are absent, and important reliability checks are acknowledged only in evaluation. Overall the methodology is âgenerally relevantâ¦ with some evidence of informed selectionâ, matching band 3-4.

Terminology such as âinelastic demandâ, âmonopolistic competitionâ, and âdynamic pricingâ is present, yet graphs have labelling issues and elasticity discussion is sometimes imprecise. Occasional misuse of symbols (Îµ for â¬) and typographical errors reduce accuracy and consistency, fitting the 1-2 mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: The title is clear, concise, and accurately conveys the research focus on coastal proximity and hotel pricing, establishing an appropriate purpose and focus.
Refine the research question to specify measurable criteria and avoid âto what extent,â improving focus and direct connection to analysis.
Word count is correctly provided and within the 4 000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
The table of contents clearly lists headings, aiding navigation and demonstrating an appropriate structure.
Ensure all TOC entries include accurate page numbers and consistent formatting to improve coherence.
The introduction provides broad national tourism data but should link more quickly to Barcelona and luxury-hotel pricing relevance.
Footnote formatting varies; standardise citation style (e.g., superscript placement and URL formatting) for consistency.
Reiterating the RQ in body duplicates content; consider moving the full question into the introduction for sharper focus.
The hypothesis section is well-labelled and clearly links basic supply-and-demand theory to expected coastal price effects.
Demand inelasticity is introduced correctly, but deeper explanation of how elasticity is measured would strengthen disciplinary understanding.
The description of demand shifting upward on the supply curve blurs movement vs shift; clarify this distinction in economic terms.
Figures should include axis labels, units, and curve annotations to improve clarity and analytical rigor.
Monopolistic competition is accurately described; linking this market structure more explicitly to coastal proximity would deepen the analysis.
The seasonality context is well defined with clear identification of peak and low periods, supporting methodological choices.
Table formatting is inconsistent and rows overflow; adjust margins or split tables to enhance readability.
Table headers split across lines create confusion; consolidate header text or adjust column widths.
Superscript numbering in âPrice per night (â¬)8â is unclear; ensure each superscript links explicitly to a footnote.
Caption for the percentage-change chart should include axis labels, units, and legend to support interpretation.
The link between percentage change and distance is descriptive but lacks statistical testing; consider computing a correlation here.
General trend statements would benefit from reiterating the computed Pearson coefficient to strengthen analytical support.
Discuss potential confounding factors such as brand reputation or amenities when interpreting outliers like Monument Hotel.
Figure 5 needs clear labeling of D and D1 curves and axis units; current diagram could confuse readers.
Data collection heading is well-structured and clearly signals the shift to empirical methodology.
The beachfront sample includes only four hotels, reducing generalisability; acknowledge this sampling limitation in methodology.
Specify the exact criteria used on Booking.com for hotel selection (ratings, filters) to strengthen methodological transparency.
The >2 km group appears skewed by location and price variance; consider enlarging this subgroup or weighting observations.
Appropriate use of Pearsonâs r quantifies the relationship between distance and price, showing analytical depth.
Include a p-value for the Pearson coefficient to demonstrate statistical significance and strengthen evaluation.
Figure 10 lacks axis titles and legend, making the chart difficult to interpret; add labels and scales.
The discussion of average prices effectively summarises findings but could more explicitly integrate economic theory to enhance coherence.
Replace the incorrect â\epsilonâ math symbol with the standard euro symbol (â¬) for clarity and consistency.
